description_long: This is a classic bolt-action rifle with a wooden stock and metal barrel, reminiscent of mid-20th-century military firearms such as the Karabiner 98k. The stock is made from a single piece of wood, extending from the butt to the fore-end, with a smooth, slightly curved shoulder rest and a metal buttplate. The barrel is long and slender, constructed from blued or parkerized steel, and features two barrel bands securing it to the stock. The action includes a prominent bolt handle and a rear iron sight, with a simple front post sight at the muzzle. The trigger guard and magazine floorplate are metal, and the rifle appears to have an internal magazine. The overall finish is utilitarian, with visible grain on the wood and a matte sheen on the metal components.
description: A classic bolt-action rifle with a wooden stock, long metal barrel, and iron sights, featuring a utilitarian finish and traditional military styling.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the full length of the wooden stock, metal barrel, and bolt-action mechanism.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the slim profile, bolt handle, and alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile revealing the stock's grain, trigger guard, and barrel bands.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the magazine floorplate, trigger guard, and underside of the barrel.
